description: Resilient Session Persistence on Browser Refresh - Students resume their exact interview state after refresh or tab-close, with no disruption to their partner

userStories.title: Persist interview sub-phase to localStorage
userStories.description: As a student, I want my interview phase (notes/followup/profile) to survive a refresh so I don't restart the round from scratch.
acceptanceCriteria: interviewPhase (notes | followup | profile) is saved to localStorage as ws_interviewPhase on every phase change inside InterviewScreen
On mount, InterviewScreen reads ws_interviewPhase and restores the correct sub-phase instead of defaulting to 'notes'
If the stored phase is 'followup' or 'profile', the component skips the notes submission step
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist textarea content to localStorage on every keystroke
userStories.description: As a student (interviewer), I want my notes and follow-up text preserved locally so a refresh doesn't lose my work.
acceptanceCriteria: notesText is saved to localStorage key ws_notesText on every input event
followupText is saved to localStorage key ws_followupText on every input event
On mount, InterviewScreen restores notesText and followupText from localStorage
When advancing to the next round (handleNextRound), both localStorage keys are cleared
When leaving the session (handleLeave, handleSwitchSession), both keys are cleared
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add beforeunload warning for unsaved work
userStories.description: As a student, I want to be warned before accidentally closing or refreshing the tab when I have unsaved interview notes.
acceptanceCriteria: A beforeunload event listener is active when screen === 'interview' and either textarea has content
The browser shows its native 'Leave site?' confirmation dialog
The listener is removed when leaving the interview screen or when the session completes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Clear stale localStorage on session end or room change
userStories.description: As a student, I don't want leftover localStorage from a previous session to interfere with joining a new one.
acceptanceCriteria: handleLeave, handleSwitchSession, and handleChangeRoom clear all ws_* keys including ws_interviewPhase, ws_notesText, ws_followupText
The reset() method in the interview store clears ws_interviewPhase, ws_notesText, ws_followupText
When the session ends (server returns ended: true), ephemeral keys are cleared
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Include submissions and AI data in workshop-room response
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the workshop-room GET endpoint to return submissions, aiFollowUps, and capabilityProfiles so the client can restore mid-round state on refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: workshop-room GET response includes the submissions array from the room blob
workshop-room GET response includes the aiFollowUps object from the room blob
workshop-room GET response includes the capabilityProfiles object from the room blob
Existing clients that don't use these fields are not broken
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Check netlify/functions/workshop-room.js — the room blob already has this data. If the function filters fields before responding, add them to the response.

userStories.title: Fix resume path to call workshop-join for re-registration
userStories.description: As a student who refreshed, I want to skip entry/room-picker and land back in my interview by re-registering via workshop-join on mount.
acceptanceCriteria: On mount, if sessionId, roomId, and studentName exist in localStorage, the resume path calls workshop-join (not just workshop-room) to re-register the student
workshop-join returns rejoined: true and the full room state including currentRound
The resume path then calls workshop-room to fetch full state including submissions and AI data
The student lands directly on the interview or waiting screen (never entry or rooms)
Students, round, role, and partnerName are restored from the server response
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the core rejoin fix. The current onMount resume path at line ~292 in +page.svelte only calls workshop-room. It should call workshop-join first, then workshop-room for full data.

userStories.title: Restore follow-up questions and profile data from server on resume
userStories.description: As a student who refreshed mid-followup or mid-profile, I want to see the AI-generated follow-up questions and profile data that were already generated.
acceptanceCriteria: On resume, if ws_interviewPhase is 'followup', the client reads aiFollowUps from the workshop-room response and displays them without re-generating
On resume, if ws_interviewPhase is 'profile', the client reads capabilityProfiles for the current round from workshop-room response and displays the profile card
If server has no stored AI data for the current round, fall back to the notes phase gracefully
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 (server returns AI data) and US-001 (interviewPhase persisted).

userStories.title: Restore notes from server when localStorage is empty (tab-close case)
userStories.description: As a student who closed their tab and returns later, I want to recover my previously auto-saved notes from the server even if localStorage was cleared.
acceptanceCriteria: On resume, if localStorage has no ws_notesText but server submissions include round{N}-notes, populate notesText with the server value
On resume, if localStorage has no ws_followupText but server submissions include round{N}-followup, populate followupText with the server value
localStorage values take priority over server values when both exist (localStorage is more recent)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 (server returns submissions in workshop-room response).

userStories.title: Ensure partner is not disrupted by a rejoin
userStories.description: As the partner of a student who refreshed, I want my session to continue without interruption — no 'waiting for partner' screen, no stale state.
acceptanceCriteria: When a student rejoins via refresh, workshop-join rejoin path does NOT remove or re-add them — it is a no-op for room state and returns the existing room
The partner's polling (workshop-room) continues to see 2 students throughout the rejoin window
The partner does not regress to the waiting screen during or after the other student's refresh
If the heartbeat lapses during refresh (>15s), there is no 'partner disconnected' state change triggered
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Mostly a verification story. workshop-join already returns the room unchanged on rejoin. The key risk is the client-side polling seeing a transient state with <2 students.
